title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis:
The requested system is analyzed from functional, performance, safety, reliability, usability, interface, manufacturing, and verification perspectives. Primary stakeholder intent: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.. Ambiguous targets should be confirmed before release; every requirement must be measurable, traceable, and independently verifiable.
clarification_questions: What operating and environmental conditions must be supported?

table.title: DFMEA – Battery-powered Handheld Vacuum Cleaner (Home Use)
rows: Provide energy for ≥20 min runtime and safe operation
Thermal runaway / overheating during use or charge
Fire, burn injury, property damage, product recall
Cell internal short, overcharge, mechanical damage, inadequate thermal design
Cell selection with safety CID/PTC; BMS OV/UV/OC/OT limits; pack mechanical protection; thermal simulation
Abuse testing (short/overcharge/crush); BMS firmware unit tests; thermal chamber validation
Add thermal fuse; increase cell spacing/venting; tighten supplier cell screening; add charge temp derating
Concept controls defined; abuse test plan drafted; BMS limits specified
Reuse BMS safety limits and abuse test matrix for future variants
Ensure compliance with IEC 62133-2 and EN 60335-1 battery clauses
Deliver rated power without nuisance shutdown
Capacity fade leading to <20 min runtime within life
High DoD cycling, high internal resistance cells, poor thermal management, aging at high SOC
Specify cell grade; DoD limits; thermal path to housing; storage SOC guidance
Cycle life test; runtime verification at 0/50/100% SOC; EOL capacity screening
Implement adaptive power management; improve airflow around pack; tighten EOL capacity spec
Initial runtime model created; draft life test protocol
Derate power at low SOC to preserve runtime; publish storage guidance
Define runtime test condition (mode, nozzle, debris load)
Generate suction airflow within performance spec
Loss of suction, overheating, possible smoke/odor
Bearing contamination, debris ingestion, winding short, insufficient cooling
Motor inlet screen; current limit; thermal cutoff; bearing spec; conformal coating where needed
End-of-line current/noise test; stall test; thermal rise test
Add improved debris screen geometry; increase motor cooling duct area; upgrade bearing seals
Baseline motor tests started; cooling duct CAD in progress
Use debris screens as standard across handheld line
Verify no user-accessible hot surfaces per EN 60335
Maintain airtight airflow path for consistent suction
Reduced suction, increased noise, runtime reduction
Poor tolerance stack-up, inadequate gasket compression, assembly error
Duct joint design with snap + seal; GD&T; assembly work instructions and fixtures
Airflow leak test at EOL; visual inspection of joint seating
Add poka-yoke features; specify gasket hardness and compression set; add in-process leak check
Seal concept chosen; draft assembly WI
Standardize leak test method and acceptance criteria
Leak spec to correlate with suction KPI
Capture fine dust and protect motor
Reduced suction, user complaints, motor overheating
Undersized filter area, high pressure drop media, lack of pre-separation, user neglect
Cyclone pre-separator; filter area sizing; user-cleanable design; maintenance indicator
Pressure drop test with standard dust; thermal test at clogged condition; user trials
Increase filter area; optimize cyclone; add filter-clean reminder; provide spare filter option
Cyclone CFD initiated; filter supplier samples requested
Specify dust loading profile for consistent comparisons
Ensure easy access without tools to encourage cleaning
Dust emission, motor contamination, reduced life
Seal deformation, improper seating, tolerance variation, latch wear
Seal design with compression stop; keyed fit; robust latch; material aging selection
EOL particle bypass test (sampling); visual check; fit gauge
Add positive seating feedback; tighten tolerances; add secondary lip seal; improve latch durability
Latch concept reviewed; seal material shortlist created
Protect internals and meet <1.5 kg target
Sharp edges, loss of function, internal damage
Insufficient wall thickness, brittle resin, stress concentrators, poor ribbing
Drop/impact design guidelines; material selection (PC/ABS); fillets/ribs; FEA
Drop test per IEC/EN household appliance methods; incoming resin certs; visual inspection
Add ribs/fillets; change to higher impact grade; add bumper features; validate with multi-orientation drops
FEA baseline completed; material datasheets collected
Apply impact design checklist early in CAD
Maintain weight target while adding ribs
Impeller imbalance, resonance, poor acoustic damping, air leakage
Balance spec; NVH design review; damping features; leak control
Sound power test in semi-anechoic; EOL vibration/current signature
Add impeller balancing step; adjust blade count; add damping pad; refine duct transitions
Initial noise baseline recorded; duct resonance analysis planned
Correlate current signature with noise for production screening
Define test mode and distance per standard
Charge safely and within specified time
Wrong PSU spec, component drift, firmware error, counterfeit adapter use
Certified PSU selection; charger IC OVP; ID handshake; labeling for correct adapter
Safety compliance testing; OVP functional test; incoming inspection for PSU
Implement adapter identification; add redundant OVP; tighten supplier control; add user warning in IFU
Charger architecture selected; compliance plan drafted
Use only certified PSUs with traceability
Must meet applicable LVD/EMC and EN 60335 charging clauses
Provide reliable electrical connection for charging
Contact corrosion, misalignment, contamination, insufficient spring force
Gold plating spec; alignment features; spring contact design; ingress shielding
EOL charge-current verification; mating cycle test; salt fog test (as applicable)
Add self-cleaning contact geometry; increase wipe length; add charge status diagnostics; improve dock guidance
Contact vendor samples received; alignment CAD updated
Consider EU common charger expectations if USB-C used
Provide clear on/off control and battery status indication
Uncontrolled operation, overheating risk, battery drain
Switch wear, contamination, PCB solder crack, latch mechanism failure
Switch life spec; sealing; debounce + watchdog; power cutoff via BMS
HALT for switch; EOL functional test; PCB AOI; firmware watchdog test
Add redundant electronic cutoff; improve switch sealing; increase mechanical life rating; add fault detection for stuck input
Watchdog implemented in prototype; switch options evaluated
Always have hardware-level power cutoff independent of UI
Accurately inform user of remaining runtime
Unexpected shutdown, poor usability, perceived low quality
Poor SoC algorithm, cell variation, temperature effects, calibration drift
Coulomb counting + OCV blend; temperature compensation; factory calibration
Runtime correlation test across temps; software unit tests; field data review plan
Add learning algorithm; tighten cell IR binning; improve low-SOC warning threshold and hysteresis
SoC algorithm baseline integrated; test matrix defined
Collect anonymized usage data for future calibration (where allowed)
Document indicator accuracy spec (±% and conditions)
Prevent dust leakage and maintain suction
Dust bin seal leaks during use
Dust emission, reduced suction, hygiene issues
Gasket set, latch not fully engaged, wear, debris on seal surface
Compression stop; gasket material spec; latch feedback; easy-to-clean sealing surfaces
EOL leak test; user assembly validation; dust emission spot checks
Add latch position indicator; change gasket to lower compression set; add secondary seal lip; improve bin guidance
Bin latch prototype built; gasket samples under test
Design seals tolerant to user contamination
Meet EU safety standards for insulation and EMC
Layout error, wrong material CTI, assembly damage, moisture ingress
PCB rules for creepage/clearance; insulation barriers; conformal coating as needed; design reviews
HiPot test; EMC pre-scan; safety compliance audit; PCB AOI
Increase creepage/slots; add insulating sheets; improve strain relief; add moisture protection where needed
Safety checklist applied to Rev A PCB; pre-scan scheduled
Embed safety rules in PCB CAD templates
Target EN 60335-1, EMC Directive, RoHS, REACH
Total mass <1.5 kg including battery and bin
Ergonomics issues, fails product requirement, market rejection
Component creep, heavy materials, late design changes, supplier substitutions
Mass budget with tracking; lightweight materials; design-to-weight reviews; supplier weight specs
Mass verification at EVT/DVT; incoming component weight checks; CAD mass properties review
Implement mass gate at each design review; revise materials; optimize ribs/fasteners; control supplier substitutions via PCN
Mass budget created; current estimate 1.43 kg with risk items listed
Maintain rolling mass budget with ownership per subsystem
Define weighing configuration (nozzle, bin empty, battery installed)
